name.text: Midterm 2 - Q4V1: TL Transient + Bounce (Resistive Load, R_L > Z0, Matched Source)

questiontext.text:
Midterm 2 – Q4V1: TL Transient + Bounce Diagram (Resistive Load, R_L > Z0, Matched Source)

Electromagnetism and Circuit Analysis — LUT University, Finland

Total: 10 points | Penalty per wrong attempt: 10%

Inspired by Ulaby Ch.2 Example 2-8 and Problems 2.32–2.38.

Scenario

A lossless transmission line with characteristic impedance \(Z_0 = 50\;\Omega\) and length \(\ell = 0.5\;\text{m}\) connects a source (\(V_g = 10\;\text{V}\), \(R_g = 50\;\Omega\)) to a resistive load \(R_L = 100\;\Omega\). The phase velocity is \(v_p = 2 \times 10^8\;\text{m/s}\).

[DIAGRAM: Transmission line system — source, line, load — to be added]

Part (a) — Reflection Coefficients and Initial Wave (2 points)

Calculate the following:

Load reflection coefficient: \(\Gamma_L = \) [[input:ans1]]   [[validation:ans1]]

Enter a number between -1 and 1, e.g. 0.789 or -0.5

Source reflection coefficient: \(\Gamma_g = \) [[input:ans2]]   [[validation:ans2]]

Enter a number, e.g. 0.333

Initial forward voltage wave: \(V_1^+ = \) [[input:ans3]] \(\text{V}\)   [[validation:ans3]]

Enter a number, e.g. 3.45

One-way travel time: \(T = \) [[input:ans4]] \(\text{s}\)   [[validation:ans4]]

Enter a number, e.g. 2.5e-9

Part (b) — Voltage at Load After Reflections (2 points)

Using the bounce diagram approach, calculate the voltage at the load at \(t = 3T\) (after the first reflected wave returns and re-reflects from the source).

\(V(\ell,\, 3T) = \) [[input:ans5]] \(\text{V}\)   [[validation:ans5]]

Enter a number, e.g. 1.23

Part (c) — TL Concepts (1 point)

Select ALL statements that are TRUE about this transmission line system:

[[input:ans6]]   [[validation:ans6]]

Part (d) — Quarter-Wave Transformer Design (3 points, manually graded)

A quarter-wave transformer (QWT) section is to be inserted between the line and load to eliminate reflections at the design frequency.

  1. What characteristic impedance \(Z_{QWT}\) should this section have?
  2. What is the physical length of the QWT section at the design frequency \(f\)?
  3. If the operating frequency shifts by 10%, does the VSWR improve or worsen? Explain qualitatively.

[[input:ans7]]   [[validation:ans7]]

generalfeedback.text:
Solution for V1

Part (a):

\(\Gamma_L = {@ta_GL@}\), \(\Gamma_g = {@ta_Gg@}\), \(V_1^+ = {@ta_V1p@}\;\text{V}\), \(T = {@ta_T@}\;\text{s}\)

Part (b): \(V(\ell, 3T) = V_1^+(1+\Gamma_L)(1+\Gamma_L\Gamma_g) = {@ta_V3T@}\;\text{V}\)

Part (c): Correct selections: {@correct_mcq@}

Part (d): \(Z_{QWT} = \sqrt{Z_0 \cdot R_L}\). Length = \(\lambda/4\) at design frequency. At 10% off, the QWT is no longer \(\lambda/4\), so \(\Gamma \neq 0\) and VSWR worsens.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Partially correct.

falsefeedback.text: Incorrect. Sum the forward and reflected waves using the bounce diagram.

name.text: Midterm 2 - Q4V2: TL Transient + Bounce (Resistive Load, R_L < Z0, Mismatched Source)

questiontext.text:
Midterm 2 – Q4V2: TL Transient + Bounce Diagram (Resistive Load, R_L < Z0, Mismatched Source)

Electromagnetism and Circuit Analysis — LUT University, Finland

Total: 10 points | Penalty per wrong attempt: 10%

Inspired by Ulaby Ch.2 Example 2-8 and Problems 2.32–2.38.

Scenario

A lossless TL (\(Z_0 = 75\;\Omega\), \(\ell = 0.3\;\text{m}\)) connects a source (\(V_g = 12\;\text{V}\), \(R_g = 50\;\Omega\)) to \(R_L = 25\;\Omega\). Phase velocity \(v_p = 2 \times 10^8\;\text{m/s}\).

[DIAGRAM: Transmission line system — source, line, load — to be added]

Part (a) — Reflection Coefficients and Initial Wave (2 points)

Calculate the following:

Load reflection coefficient: \(\Gamma_L = \) [[input:ans1]]   [[validation:ans1]]

Enter a number between -1 and 1, e.g. 0.789 or -0.5

Source reflection coefficient: \(\Gamma_g = \) [[input:ans2]]   [[validation:ans2]]

Enter a number, e.g. 0.333

Initial forward voltage wave: \(V_1^+ = \) [[input:ans3]] \(\text{V}\)   [[validation:ans3]]

Enter a number, e.g. 3.45

One-way travel time: \(T = \) [[input:ans4]] \(\text{s}\)   [[validation:ans4]]

Enter a number, e.g. 2.5e-9

Part (b) — Voltage at Load After Reflections (2 points)

Using the bounce diagram approach, calculate the voltage at the load at \(t = 3T\) (after the first reflected wave returns and re-reflects from the source).

\(V(\ell,\, 3T) = \) [[input:ans5]] \(\text{V}\)   [[validation:ans5]]

Enter a number, e.g. 1.23

Part (c) — TL Concepts (1 point)

Select ALL statements that are TRUE about this transmission line system:

[[input:ans6]]   [[validation:ans6]]

Part (d) — Quarter-Wave Transformer Design (3 points, manually graded)

A quarter-wave transformer (QWT) section is to be inserted between the line and load to eliminate reflections at the design frequency.

  1. What characteristic impedance \(Z_{QWT}\) should this section have?
  2. What is the physical length of the QWT section at the design frequency \(f\)?
  3. If the operating frequency shifts by 10%, does the VSWR improve or worsen? Explain qualitatively.

[[input:ans7]]   [[validation:ans7]]

generalfeedback.text:
Solution for V2

Part (a):

\(\Gamma_L = {@ta_GL@}\), \(\Gamma_g = {@ta_Gg@}\), \(V_1^+ = {@ta_V1p@}\;\text{V}\), \(T = {@ta_T@}\;\text{s}\)

Part (b): \(V(\ell, 3T) = V_1^+(1+\Gamma_L)(1+\Gamma_L\Gamma_g) = {@ta_V3T@}\;\text{V}\)

Part (c): Correct selections: {@correct_mcq@}

Part (d): \(Z_{QWT} = \sqrt{Z_0 \cdot R_L}\). Length = \(\lambda/4\) at design frequency. At 10% off, the QWT is no longer \(\lambda/4\), so \(\Gamma \neq 0\) and VSWR worsens.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Partially correct.

falsefeedback.text: Incorrect. Sum the forward and reflected waves using the bounce diagram.

name.text: Midterm 2 - Q4V3: TL Transient + Bounce (Short-Circuit Load, Mismatched Source)

questiontext.text:
Midterm 2 – Q4V3: TL Transient + Bounce Diagram (Short-Circuit Load, Mismatched Source)

Electromagnetism and Circuit Analysis — LUT University, Finland

Total: 10 points | Penalty per wrong attempt: 10%

Inspired by Ulaby Ch.2 Example 2-8 and Problems 2.32–2.38.

Scenario

A lossless TL (\(Z_0 = 50\;\Omega\), \(\ell = 0.4\;\text{m}\)) connects a source (\(V_g = 8\;\text{V}\), \(R_g = 100\;\Omega\)) to a short-circuit load (\(R_L \approx 0\)). Phase velocity \(v_p = 2 \times 10^8\;\text{m/s}\).

[DIAGRAM: Transmission line system — source, line, load — to be added]

Part (a) — Reflection Coefficients and Initial Wave (2 points)

Calculate the following:

Load reflection coefficient: \(\Gamma_L = \) [[input:ans1]]   [[validation:ans1]]

Enter a number between -1 and 1, e.g. 0.789 or -0.5

Source reflection coefficient: \(\Gamma_g = \) [[input:ans2]]   [[validation:ans2]]

Enter a number, e.g. 0.333

Initial forward voltage wave: \(V_1^+ = \) [[input:ans3]] \(\text{V}\)   [[validation:ans3]]

Enter a number, e.g. 3.45

One-way travel time: \(T = \) [[input:ans4]] \(\text{s}\)   [[validation:ans4]]

Enter a number, e.g. 2.5e-9

Part (b) — Voltage at Load After Reflections (2 points)

Using the bounce diagram approach, calculate the voltage at the load at \(t = 3T\) (after the first reflected wave returns and re-reflects from the source).

\(V(\ell,\, 3T) = \) [[input:ans5]] \(\text{V}\)   [[validation:ans5]]

Enter a number, e.g. 1.23

Part (c) — TL Concepts (1 point)

Select ALL statements that are TRUE about this transmission line system:

[[input:ans6]]   [[validation:ans6]]

Part (d) — Quarter-Wave Transformer Design (3 points, manually graded)

A quarter-wave transformer (QWT) section is to be inserted between the line and load to eliminate reflections at the design frequency.

  1. What characteristic impedance \(Z_{QWT}\) should this section have?
  2. What is the physical length of the QWT section at the design frequency \(f\)?
  3. If the operating frequency shifts by 10%, does the VSWR improve or worsen? Explain qualitatively.

[[input:ans7]]   [[validation:ans7]]

generalfeedback.text:
Solution for V3

Part (a):

\(\Gamma_L = {@ta_GL@}\), \(\Gamma_g = {@ta_Gg@}\), \(V_1^+ = {@ta_V1p@}\;\text{V}\), \(T = {@ta_T@}\;\text{s}\)

Part (b): \(V(\ell, 3T) = V_1^+(1+\Gamma_L)(1+\Gamma_L\Gamma_g) = {@ta_V3T@}\;\text{V}\)

Part (c): Correct selections: {@correct_mcq@}

Part (d): \(Z_{QWT} = \sqrt{Z_0 \cdot R_L}\). Length = \(\lambda/4\) at design frequency. At 10% off, the QWT is no longer \(\lambda/4\), so \(\Gamma \neq 0\) and VSWR worsens.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Partially correct.

falsefeedback.text: Incorrect. Sum the forward and reflected waves using the bounce diagram.

name.text: Midterm 2 - Q4V4: TL Transient + Bounce (Open-Circuit Load, Matched Source)

questiontext.text:
Midterm 2 – Q4V4: TL Transient + Bounce Diagram (Open-Circuit Load, Matched Source)

Electromagnetism and Circuit Analysis — LUT University, Finland

Total: 10 points | Penalty per wrong attempt: 10%

Inspired by Ulaby Ch.2 Example 2-8 and Problems 2.32–2.38.

Scenario

A lossless TL (\(Z_0 = 50\;\Omega\), \(\ell = 0.6\;\text{m}\)) connects a source (\(V_g = 6\;\text{V}\), \(R_g = 50\;\Omega\)) to an open-circuit load (\(R_L \to \infty\)). Phase velocity \(v_p = 2 \times 10^8\;\text{m/s}\).

[DIAGRAM: Transmission line system — source, line, load — to be added]

Part (a) — Reflection Coefficients and Initial Wave (2 points)

Calculate the following:

Load reflection coefficient: \(\Gamma_L = \) [[input:ans1]]   [[validation:ans1]]

Enter a number between -1 and 1, e.g. 0.789 or -0.5

Source reflection coefficient: \(\Gamma_g = \) [[input:ans2]]   [[validation:ans2]]

Enter a number, e.g. 0.333

Initial forward voltage wave: \(V_1^+ = \) [[input:ans3]] \(\text{V}\)   [[validation:ans3]]

Enter a number, e.g. 3.45

One-way travel time: \(T = \) [[input:ans4]] \(\text{s}\)   [[validation:ans4]]

Enter a number, e.g. 2.5e-9

Part (b) — Voltage at Load After Reflections (2 points)

Using the bounce diagram approach, calculate the voltage at the load at \(t = 3T\) (after the first reflected wave returns and re-reflects from the source).

\(V(\ell,\, 3T) = \) [[input:ans5]] \(\text{V}\)   [[validation:ans5]]

Enter a number, e.g. 1.23

Part (c) — TL Concepts (1 point)

Select ALL statements that are TRUE about this transmission line system:

[[input:ans6]]   [[validation:ans6]]

Part (d) — Quarter-Wave Transformer Design (3 points, manually graded)

A quarter-wave transformer (QWT) section is to be inserted between the line and load to eliminate reflections at the design frequency.

  1. What characteristic impedance \(Z_{QWT}\) should this section have?
  2. What is the physical length of the QWT section at the design frequency \(f\)?
  3. If the operating frequency shifts by 10%, does the VSWR improve or worsen? Explain qualitatively.

[[input:ans7]]   [[validation:ans7]]

generalfeedback.text:
Solution for V4

Part (a):

\(\Gamma_L = {@ta_GL@}\), \(\Gamma_g = {@ta_Gg@}\), \(V_1^+ = {@ta_V1p@}\;\text{V}\), \(T = {@ta_T@}\;\text{s}\)

Part (b): \(V(\ell, 3T) = V_1^+(1+\Gamma_L)(1+\Gamma_L\Gamma_g) = {@ta_V3T@}\;\text{V}\)

Part (c): Correct selections: {@correct_mcq@}

Part (d): \(Z_{QWT} = \sqrt{Z_0 \cdot R_L}\). Length = \(\lambda/4\) at design frequency. At 10% off, the QWT is no longer \(\lambda/4\), so \(\Gamma \neq 0\) and VSWR worsens.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Partially correct.

falsefeedback.text: Incorrect. Sum the forward and reflected waves using the bounce diagram.

name.text: Midterm 2 - Q4: Upload Handwritten Work (TL Transient)

questiontext.text:
Upload Handwritten Work for Q4

Upload work for Question 4 (TL Transient + Bounce Diagram).

Include:

  • Reflection coefficient calculations
  • Bounce diagram with voltage labels at each bounce
  • Summation work for V(l, 3T)
  • QWT impedance calculation and frequency reasoning (Part d)

Up to 2 files: PDF, JPG, JPEG, PNG. Worth 0 points.

Teacher notice: Set Allow attachments to 2+ in quiz settings.

graderinfo.text:
• Check bounce diagram completeness
  • Verify QWT design: Z_QWT = sqrt(Z0*RL)
